summary: The test suite comprehensively covers all five public functions against every stated requirement: process object creation, threshold filtering (CPU-only, memory-only, OR-combined, zero thresholds, no-match), top-N selection (both sort axes, count overflow, invalid inputs), report generation (populated and empty), the full end-to-end pipeline, and the mockable data-source reader. Error paths (negative values, empty name, invalid SortBy, Count<1) are all exercised with throw assertions. Rigor is solid but not perfect: the whitespace-only name edge case is untested despite the implementation using IsNullOrWhiteSpace; boundary equality (value exactly at threshold) is exercised incidentally via the zero-threshold test but never explicitly for a non-zero threshold; and the end-to-end tests verify presence of names but not exact count or ordering of the TopN slice. Design is clean — a shared New-MockProcessList factory lives in BeforeAll, Describe-scoped BeforeAll blocks provide fresh $script:procs for each group, and test names read as specifications. Minor structural concerns: the 'Edge cases' Describe block conflates two independent scenarios that belong inside their respective functional Describe blocks, and some integration assertions could be tighter (e.g., asserting HaveCount 2 after -TopN 2 in Invoke-ProcessMonitor). Overall this is a trustworthy suite that would catch the vast majority of regressions in the implementation.
